steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists three deserts in a series: the Chihuahuan, the Karakum, and the Taklamakan. Each item has an internal comma between the desert's name and its ranking. When list items contain internal commas, Standard English requires semicolons to separate the items.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — eleventh in size; the Karakum Desert; ranked twelfth;: places a semicolon between "Karakum Desert" and "ranked twelfth," splitting the name from its ranking instead of keeping them together. ✗
  • Choice B — eleventh in size the Karakum Desert, ranked twelfth: no semicolons anywhere, so the three items run together without proper separation. ✗
  • Choice C — eleventh in size, the Karakum Desert; ranked twelfth,: uses a comma after "eleventh in size," which blurs the boundary between the first and second list items. ✗
  • Choice D — eleventh in size; the Karakum Desert, ranked twelfth;: semicolons separate the three list items, and the comma correctly pairs "Karakum Desert" with "ranked twelfth" within that item. ✓
D is correct.
